skill.name: senior-fullstack
skill.description: Guides fullstack project planning, architecture review, stack selection, and basic local report generation for modern web applications.
skill.summary: Plan, review, and document fullstack web application architecture and workflows.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: The static findings are mostly false positives from markdown command examples, TypeScript snippets, and generic words that were misread as weak cryptography. The only confirmed behavior is local JSON report writing to a user-provided output path, with no evidence of network access, subprocess execution, credential theft, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Documented Shell Commands Are False Positive Execution Alerts
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged external command locations are markdown examples and usage instructions, not code paths that execute commands. Users should still review copied commands before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced files contain fenced bash or TypeScript examples and no runtime command execution. I found no subprocess, shell, or network code behind these documentation examples.

low_findings.title: User-Selected Local Output File Writes
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE with low severity. The Python scripts write JSON output when a user passes --json and --output. This is expected reporting behavior, but it can overwrite a path chosen by the user.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write behavior is explicit and only uses the user-supplied output argument. There is no evidence of hidden file discovery, secret collection, or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Environment File Reference Is Documentation Only
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The environment access finding points to a documented cp .env.example .env setup command. No script reads environment files or sends secrets elsewhere.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a setup instruction in markdown, not code that opens or parses .env files. The residual concern is only that users should avoid committing copied secret files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Have No Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged weak cryptography locations do not contain cryptographic APIs, hash functions, encryption, password storage, or security-sensitive token generation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found generic descriptive text and argparse metadata at these lines. No cryptographic operation is present in the reviewed evidence.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Generic Reference Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The system reconnaissance alerts point to generic anti-pattern or documentation text, not commands that enumerate host, network, user, or process information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are headings or generic guidance. I found no reconnaissance commands, filesystem crawling, or environment enumeration behavior.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent guidance when planning fullstack applications across frontend, backend, database, and deployment layers. This skill provides structured prompts, reference patterns, and simple local scripts for project review workflows.
actual_capabilities: Guides fullstack stack selection across React, Next.js, Node.js, GraphQL, PostgreSQL, cloud, and DevOps tools.
Provides reference documentation for architecture patterns, development workflows, and technology stack decisions.
Lists common development, analysis, and deployment commands for local project workflows.
Includes Python CLI scripts that validate a target path and print a simple status report.
Can emit a JSON report file when a script is run with --json and --output.
limitations: The included Python scripts are placeholder analyzers and do not perform deep code analysis.
The reference guides contain generic examples instead of framework-specific implementation details.
The skill does not install dependencies, deploy infrastructure, or create complete production projects by itself.
Users must review shell commands and generated plans before applying them to a real repository.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Web App
use_cases.description: Create a first-pass architecture plan with frontend, backend, database, and deployment choices.

use_cases.title: Review Project Structure
use_cases.description: Check a repository plan against common organization, performance, security, and maintainability guidelines.

use_cases.title: Prepare Development Workflow
use_cases.description: Document setup, quality checks, and common commands for a fullstack application team.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Stack
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a fullstack web stack for a small application. Include frontend, backend, database, hosting, and testing choices.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Project Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a project structure for a Next.js and Node.js application with PostgreSQL. Include folders, responsibilities, and setup steps.

prompt_templates.title: Review Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this fullstack architecture for scalability, security, maintainability, and deployment risk. Recommend concrete improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Define Delivery Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a development workflow for a fullstack team. Include local setup, quality checks, pull request review, CI, deployment, and rollback practices.

output_examples.input: Plan a stack for a B2B dashboard with teams, billing, and reporting.
output_examples.output: A concise architecture plan covering Next.js, Node.js APIs, PostgreSQL, authentication, background jobs, hosting, testing, and deployment checks.

output_examples.input: Review our proposed app structure before implementation.
output: Highlights missing boundaries between UI, API, persistence, and infrastructure.
Recommends clearer testing, security, and observability steps.
Flags areas that need team decisions before coding starts.

output_examples.input: Create onboarding commands for a new fullstack repository.
output_examples.output: A setup checklist with dependency installation, environment file preparation, local development commands, linting, tests, and build verification.

best_practices: Use the skill for planning and review before making large repository changes.
Validate all generated commands in a disposable branch or local sandbox first.
Pair the generic guidance with project-specific framework documentation.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the placeholder scripts as a complete code quality scanner.
Do not run deployment or infrastructure commands without reviewing the target environment.
Do not copy environment files into version control or shared outputs.

faq.question: Does this skill build a complete application automatically?
faq.answer: No. It mainly provides planning guidance, generic references, command examples, and simple local reporting scripts.

faq.question: Can it analyze an existing repository?
faq.answer: It can guide a review process, but the included scripts only validate a path and emit a basic status report.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No evidence of network access was found in the reviewed files.

faq.question: Why is the security risk low instead of safe?
faq.answer: The Python scripts can write a JSON report to a user-selected output path, so users should choose paths carefully.

faq.question: What should users review before running commands?
faq.answer: Users should review package installation, environment file, Docker, Kubernetes, and deployment commands for their own project context.
